title: axonopus centralis
intro: Axonopus centralis, also known as Central Axonopus or Axonopus, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Central and South America, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Axonopus centralis has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The plant is often prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to utilize its medicinal properties.
